title: Due Scheduling, Monotonic Clocks, Backoff, Jitter, and Pause Policy · Runtime Contract: Feed Go Backoff Jitter
summary: A ticker polls every feed at one interval, wall-clock jumps duplicate work, failures retry in lockstep, successful publishers never adapt, and paused sources remain due. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go backoff jitter increment in deterministic adaptive polling scheduler. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…
objectives: Apply bounded exponential backoff with deterministic-testable jitter, reset rules, Retry-After, and maximum staleness escalation.
Inject wall and monotonic time roles, define inclusive due comparisons, and test forward, backward, and boundary changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Backoff Jitter · Theory Feed Go Backoff Jitter
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 9090 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove persisted due state, injected clocks, adaptive backoff, jitter, pause, and non-overlap; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
